name: TiDB
description: Open-source, cloud-native, distributed SQL database designed for HTAP workloads with MySQL compatibility

license.comment: Fully open source under Apache 2.0 license, including enterprise features. TiKV (storage layer) is a CNCF graduated project.

hosting-options.comment: TiDB Cloud offers Serverless (Starter), Dedicated, and Self-Managed options. Serverless includes generous free tier.

initial-release.comment: PingCAP founded in 2015. Repository created September 2015. Inspired by Google Spanner and F1 papers.

primary-language.comment: TiDB (SQL layer) is written in Go. TiKV (storage layer) is written in Rust.

data-model.comment: Relational database with HTAP (Hybrid Transactional and Analytical Processing) capabilities.

topology.comment: Uses Raft consensus for each Region (data chunk). Each Region has a leader that handles reads/writes.

sharding.comment: Data automatically sharded into Regions (ranges of rows). Regions are split and redistributed dynamically as data grows.

replication-factor.comment: Default 3 replicas per Region. Configurable; recommended 5 replicas for geo-distributed deployments.

multi-region.comment: Native support for geo-distributed deployments. Supports 3 AZs in 2 cities topology with placement rules for data locality.

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency using Raft consensus. Strong consistency maintained even during node failures via quorum.

consistency-model.comment: Strong consistency using Raft consensus for replication. Implements snapshot isolation with Percolator protocol.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID support using 2PC protocol based on Percolator. Both optimistic and pessimistic transaction modes supported.

transaction-scope.comment: Transactions can span multiple nodes and Regions across the entire cluster.

isolation-levels.comment: Default is Snapshot Isolation (advertised as Repeatable Read for MySQL compatibility). Read Committed available in pessimistic mode. Serializable supported.

distributed-transactions.comment: Uses two-phase commit (2PC) based on Percolator protocol with MVCC for distributed transaction coordination.

sql-compatibility.comment: MySQL wire protocol compatible. Most MySQL drivers and tools work without modification. Some feature differences documented.

secondary-indexes.comment: Full secondary index support including composite indexes. Indexes are global and distributed across nodes.

joins.comment: Full distributed JOIN support. TiDB's Co-Processor pushes join operations to TiKV nodes when possible.

aggregations.comment: Full aggregation support with pre-aggregation pushed down to TiKV nodes for performance.

full-text-search.comment: Native full-text search using inverted indexes. Fully integrated with SQL including joins, filters, and aggregations.

max-cluster-size.comment: Computing layer supports max 512 nodes. Cluster capacity scales to petabyte level.

write-throughput.value: TPC-C benchmark, scales linearly with nodes
write-throughput.comment: Performance scales near-linearly with TiDB nodes (slightly sub-linear beyond 8 nodes). Official TPC-C benchmarks available.

read-latency-p99.comment: P99 latency ~84ms in example TPC-C workloads. Average latency ~10.8ms. Varies by workload and configuration.

auto-scaling.comment: TiDB Cloud Serverless auto-scales including scale-to-zero. Self-hosted supports adding/removing nodes without downtime.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Automatic Region splitting and rebalancing via PD (Placement Driver). Load-based scheduling distributes hot Regions.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades supported. TiProxy provides seamless session migration during TiDB server reboots.

online-schema-changes.comment: Online DDL based on Google F1 design. Schema changes run incrementally with zero downtime.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: PITR via log backup with RPO as short as 5 minutes. Restores full snapshot plus log replay to target timestamp.

backup-strategy.comment: BR tool supports full/incremental snapshots. Continuous log backup for PITR. TiCDC for streaming changes.

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official TiDB Operator by PingCAP. Open-sourced in August 2018. Supports AWS EKS, GKE, and other K8s platforms.

rust-driver.comment: Uses MySQL-compatible Rust drivers via MySQL wire protocol. No official TiDB-specific driver.

go-driver.comment: Uses go-sql-driver/mysql via MySQL wire protocol. Compatible with GORM ORM.

java-driver.comment: TiDB-JDBC is a customized driver based on MySQL 8.0.29 with TiDB-specific features. Standard MySQL JDBC also works.

dotnet-driver.comment: Uses MySQL Connector/NET via MySQL wire protocol compatibility.

python-driver.comment: Uses PyMySQL, mysqlclient, or MySQL Connector/Python. django-tidb dialect available for Django ORM.

ruby-driver.comment: Uses mysql2 gem via MySQL wire protocol compatibility.

c-cpp-driver.comment: Uses MySQL Connector/C++ via MySQL wire protocol compatibility.

orm-support.comment: Supports major ORMs via MySQL compatibility. django-tidb provides Django ORM dialect. SQLAlchemy, GORM, Hibernate, Sequelize, peewee supported.

open-source.comment: Fully open source under Apache 2.0 license including all enterprise features. TiKV is a CNCF graduated project.

free-tier.comment: TiDB Cloud Serverless includes free quota: 5 GiB row + 5 GiB columnar storage + 50M Request Units per cluster, up to 5 clusters per org.

pricing-model.comment: Serverless uses per-request (RU) + per-storage. Dedicated uses traditional node-based pricing.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Serverless free tier generous for development. Dedicated pricing varies by configuration and cloud provider.

github-stars.comment: As of December 2024. One of the most starred database projects on GitHub.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Stack Overflow activity is low; community primarily uses TiDB Forum (ask.pingcap.com) and Discord.

change-data-capture.comment: TiCDC streams changes to Kafka, Pulsar, MySQL, TiDB, and cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob). Supports Canal-JSON, Avro, and Debezium formats.

watch-subscribe.comment: No native client subscription API. Real-time notifications require consuming TiCDC changefeeds via Kafka/Pulsar. Community tipubsub library provides pub/sub pattern using TiDB as backend.

triggers.comment: TiDB does not support triggers, stored procedures, or user-defined functions. Server-side logic must be implemented at the application layer.

event-streaming.comment: TiCDC supports native Kafka and Pulsar sinks. Pulsar support improved in v7.5.1/v8.0.0 with OAuth2 and TLS. No native Kinesis support.
